title: senecio gnoma
intro: Senecio gnoma, also known as devil's weed or annual ragwort, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a purgative and to treat various health conditions. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Senecio gnoma can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for various ailments.
